title: Roku Q1 2024 Earnings Report
text: Roku exceeded Wall Street expectations in its first-quarter 2024 earnings announcement on April 25, 2024. The streaming platform company reported adjusted losses of 35 cents per share on $882 million in revenue, surpassing analyst forecasts of 61 cents per share losses on $848.62 million. The company grew its active user base to 81.6 million accounts, adding 1.6 million users from the previous quarter. Streaming hours climbed to 30.8 billion, up from 29.1 billion in Q4 2023. Platform revenue reached $754.9 million, while device sales contributed $126.5 million. Gross profit increased 15% year-over-year to $388.3 million. Free cash flow remained stable at $426.8 million compared to the prior year. The company's net loss narrowed significantly to $50.9 million, improving from a $193.6 million loss in the same period last year. For Q2, Roku projects net revenue of $935 million and gross profit of $410 million, though it anticipates a net loss of $65 million. Management noted challenges ahead, stating: "We face difficult year-over-year growth rate comparisons" due to prior price increases and the shift toward ad-supported streaming services. Stock performance was positive, closing at $62.81 per share and rising 6% in after-hours trading.
